should_trigger: We have 30,000 agent run logs with trace ids, tool calls, timestamps, retries, and final status. Discover the actual workflow variants and identify where the orchestrator gets stuck.
Compare the observed traces from our onboarding workflow with the intended state machine. Which steps are skipped, repeated, or happening out of order?
Design the event schema we need so future agent runs can be mined for bottlenecks, rework loops, conformance violations, and reusable successful templates.
Use last month's failed task traces to find common failure paths and turn them into preflight checks, retry rules, and escalation triggers.
should_not_trigger: Create a one-page policy document describing how our customer approval process should work, even though we have no runtime logs yet.
Debug this single stack trace from a failed API call and tell me the immediate code fix.
Explain process mining at a high level for a nontechnical audience without applying it to any orchestration design.
edge_cases: We only have free-text logs and no stable trace id. Decide whether process mining is feasible, what can be inferred safely, and what instrumentation must be added first.

The logs show agents often skip manual review, but those traces also finish faster and have fewer failures. Determine whether this is a conformance bug or a useful variant before changing the orchestrator.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Activate Process Mining and produce an evidence-first report covering data contract, event quality, variants, conformance, bottlenecks, representative trace evidence, design feedback, and validation checks.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Do not activate for policy-only process design, single-stack-trace debugging, or generic explanations without event logs, traces, or instrumentation for process reconstruction.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: Gate conclusions on case-id and activity quality, quarantine weak traces, distinguish useful variants from defects, and avoid causal claims without validation.
